title: Run self-improvement workflow as atomic memory sync
decision: Execute self-improvement workflow runs as one cohesive chain that updates patterns, iteration history, project context, and decisions in the same session.
rationale: Partial updates create stale or inconsistent memory artifacts and reduce the reliability of follow-up automation.

alternatives_considered.option: Update only selected memory files ad-hoc
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Increases drift risk and weakens reproducibility of future runs.

title: Self-improvement workflow is composed from 5 base skills
decision: Treat self-improvement as an orchestrated workflow that chains session-bootstrap, pattern-extractor, iteration-logger, context-keeper, and skill-generator.
rationale: A standalone self-improvement skill was not installed, but all required primitives already exist and are stable.

alternatives_considered.option: Wait for a separate single skill package
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would block immediate usage even though all building blocks are available now.

title: Backlog expanded with runtime hardening priorities
decision: Add priorities P19-P23 to address async route blocking, pending-hit lifecycle, calibration modularization, multi-cam fusion burst handling, and app-state concurrency contracts.
rationale: These risks were directly observed during current codebase analysis and coverage verification.

alternatives_considered.option: Continue only with existing backlog items
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Would leave currently observed runtime risks undocumented and unprioritized.
